verdict_reason: Solid, opinionated API design guide covering Hyrum's Law, contract-first design, error semantics, and TypeScript patterns with clear red flags and a verification checklist — worth a full Level 2a scorecard to assess fit against David's RVETS/Signal Studio stack.

Exceptional skill with clear mechanism (Chrome DevTools MCP), comprehensive debugging workflows, strong security boundary guidance for prompt injection via browser content, and an immediately actionable verification checklist — directly applicable to David's frontend work across FliVideo, Signal Studio, and other browser-facing apps.

Solid, opinionated CI/CD reference with practical GitHub Actions patterns, shift-left rationale, agent feedback loop integration, and a useful "common rationalizations" table — strong enough for a full Level 2a scorecard to determine if it adds value beyond what David's existing delivery tooling already covers.

Exceptionally thorough multi-axis framework (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) with concrete checklists, severity labeling, multi-model review pattern, and anti-rationalization guidance — directly applicable to David's agentic stack and could skip straight to distillation.

Exceptional depth — five named principles, concrete before/after examples across TS/Python/React, explicit red flags, a full verification checklist, and strong guard rails (Chesterton's Fence, Rule of 500, scope discipline) make this immediately usable and well-aligned with David's codebase quality standards.

Exceptional, mechanism-rich treatment of context curation (5-level hierarchy, packing strategies, confusion management, anti-patterns) that is directly foundational to David's agent stack and warrants a full deep eval.

Strong stop-the-line triage process with clear decision trees, root-cause discipline, regression-test guarding, and a prompt-injection guard on error output — worth a full deep eval, though npm/TS examples need de-stacking.

Strong, well-structured skill with concrete decision framework, named migration patterns (strangler/adapter/feature-flag), the Churn Rule, and zombie-code diagnostics — stack-agnostic and worth a full deep eval.

Strong, well-structured ADR/documentation discipline with clear why-not-what mechanism, lifecycle, anti-patterns, and explicit agent-context considerations — worth a deep eval, though it overlaps David's existing knowledge-capture/system-context skills and carries TS-specific examples to strip.

Exceptional, mechanism-rich skill defining in-flight adversarial fresh-context review with anti-bias rules, bounded loops, reconciliation precedence, cross-model escalation safety properties, and checkable failure signals — directly strengthens David's verification/delivery-review stack and could go straight to distillation.
